etymology.description: semantic 子 (child; son) + phonetic 皿 (sound) -> surname Meng
etymology.interpretation: The character 孟 (Mèng) combines 子 (zǐ, 'child; son') as the semantic component representing a generation or lineage, with 皿 (mǐn, as sound provider) as the phonetic element. It traditionally indicates the eldest in the family, such as 'firstborn' or 'eldest son', later used as a surname.

examples.chinese: 孟家在这个村子里非常有名(孟家在這個村子裏非常有名)。
examples.pinyin: Mèng jiā zài zhège cūnzi lǐ fēicháng yǒumíng.
examples.english: The Meng family is very famous in this village.

examples.chinese: 孟先生是一位出色的作家(孟先生是一位出色的作家)。
examples.pinyin: Mèng xiānshēng shì yī wèi chūsè de zuòjiā.
examples.english: Mr. Meng is an outstanding writer.

examples.chinese: 我们去拜访孟叔叔时(我們去拜訪孟叔叔時)，他正在读书(他正在讀書)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen qù bàifǎng Mèng shūshu shí, tā zhèngzài dúshū.
examples.english: When we visited Uncle Meng, he was reading a book.
